knownIssues.title: Separate PC adapter required (not included)
knownIssues.description: The PSVR2 requires Sony's official PC Adapter ($60) to work with a PC. It does not connect directly via USB-C.
knownIssues.workaround: Purchase the Sony PSVR2 PC Adapter. It converts the PSVR2's USB-C output to DisplayPort for your GPU.

knownIssues.title: Some PSVR2 features don't work on PC
knownIssues.description: HDR, haptic feedback in headset, adaptive triggers, and headset rumble are not available when using PSVR2 on PC. Eye tracking has limited game support.
knownIssues.workaround: These are hardware limitations of the PC adapter. Eye tracking works in some SteamVR titles that support it. Haptics and HDR are PS5-only features.

knownIssues.title: Controller pairing requires Bluetooth
knownIssues.description: PSVR2 Sense controllers connect via Bluetooth, not through the adapter. Your PC needs a Bluetooth 4.0 adapter.
knownIssues.workaround: If your PC lacks built-in Bluetooth, purchase a USB Bluetooth 4.0+ adapter. Pair controllers through Windows Bluetooth settings.

optimizationTips.tip: OLED panels enable eye-tracked foveated rendering
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Some games can use the PSVR2's eye tracking to focus graphics power where you're looking, making things run smoother.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: The PSVR2's eye tracking enables foveated rendering in supported SteamVR OpenXR titles. When available, this reduces peripheral resolution by 30-50%, providing significant GPU savings. Check per-game OpenXR settings for foveated rendering options.

optimizationTips.tip: OLED panels provide superior contrast for dark environments
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: The PSVR2's OLED screen makes dark scenes in VR look incredible with true black levels and vivid colors.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: OLED's per-pixel illumination eliminates backlight bleed and provides infinite contrast ratio. Set SteamVR brightness to default. In-game gamma settings at default or slightly lower than LCD headsets will take advantage of the OLED's native contrast. Mura correction is handled by the headset firmware.
